task_completion.description: Log in to the Aerobridge admin panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the web browser navigated to http://localhost:8000/admin/ with the login form filled using 'admin' or the Aerobridge Django admin dashboard visible after successful login.

task_completion.description: Identify aircraft missing a Type Certificate
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the Aircraft list view in the admin panel, where the agent is inspecting aircraft records (like 'Project X-1 Prototype', 'Surveyor Alpha', or 'Cargo Lifter V2') that lack a Type Certificate.
Screenshot showing the 'Add Type certificate' form in the Django admin being filled out with a realistic ID (e.g., TC-2024-XXX), valid dates, and a manufacturer.

task_completion.description: Assign the new Type Certificates to the uncertified aircraft
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the 'Change Aircraft' form in the Django admin where the previously uncertified aircraft is being updated by selecting a newly created Type Certificate from the dropdown menu and saving.

task_completion.description: Create a compliance report text file listing the remediated aircraft
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a text editor (e.g., nano, gedit) or terminal command creating a file at '/home/ga/compliance_report.txt' that contains the names of the aircraft that were assigned new certificates.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the admin panel
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Firefox) open and navigating the Django admin interface, rather than the agent using curl or python scripts in the terminal to make HTTP requests.

integrity.description: The agent manually created and assigned records via the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Django admin HTML forms to add Type Certificates and modify Aircraft, rather than directly modifying the SQLite database via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The compliance report was genuinely saved to the requested path
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the file being explicitly saved to '/home/ga/compliance_report.txt' in an editor or via a terminal redirect, not just printed to standard output or saved in a temporary/incorrect directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Admin URL: http://localhost:8000/admin/. Credentials: username 'admin', password 'adminpass123'. The synthetic registry contains 3 uncertified target aircraft that need remediation: 'Project X-1 Prototype', 'Surveyor Alpha', and 'Cargo Lifter V2'. The final report must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/compliance_report.txt'.
